task.title: UI polish: P0 hover text color + sticky green on selected approval/option

messages.content:
Two small CSS polish fixes in S-Deck. Both in public/style.css (or wherever the relevant rules live).

Worktree: .synapse/worktrees/developer-37-ui-polish-p0-approval
Branch: synapse/developer-37-ui-polish-p0-approval
cd into it first; commit there; do not push.

---

Fix 1 — P0 message hover color
Problem: When hovering over a P0 (priority 0, urgent) message row, the text color changes to black. Operator wants it to stay dark red on hover (P0's normal color).
Action: Find the P0 message hover rule(s) (likely a `.message-row.p0:hover` or similar) and remove/override the `color` change so it inherits the dark red from the non-hover P0 rule. Confirm both light and dark themes.

Recent context: commits `876c89a` (light-theme polish: P0 emphasis), `b2ce903` (P0 styling: tone down text color and hover background) are the most relevant prior work. Look there.

---

Fix 2 — Approval/option label sticky green after selection
Problem: When the operator clicks an Approve button or selects one of the request_options buttons, the green selected/active styling disappears once the click resolves. Operator wants the chosen option/approve button to stay green so they can see what was picked.
Action: Locate the click handler for approval buttons (likely in public/app.js — search "approve", "request_options", or "needs_approval"). After click resolution, add a class (e.g. `selected` / `picked`) to the clicked button, and add a CSS rule keeping it green. The non-picked options can fade or hide.
Recent context: commits `3fb4846` (approve quick-reply button), `831caa1` (needs_approval scope) are relevant.

---

Steps:
1. cd into the worktree
2. Make both fixes (one or two commits — your call, but keep each commit focused)
3. Reload S-Deck and visually confirm if you can; if not, describe the expected before/after CSS rule for each fix in your DONE
4. report_done with file:line for each change

If either issue isn't where I expect, say so in DONE and explain what you found.
